events.title: Men's Tennis vs Purchase
events.description: Tennis game!

events.title: Conservatory Orchestra Concert
events.description: String orchestra, Students free admission with ID.

events.title: ASL Basics Lesson #1
events.description:
In this event we will be introducing how to sign the alphabets and numbers using American Sign Language. 
We will also be going over how to spell out your name and how to introduce yourself using sign language.
Food Provided (We will be providing Pizza and Drinks! )

events.title: Playing with Puzzles - Chess Club
events.description: Sharpen your skills at this event centered around puzzles! Work through tactical problems ranging from beginner to advanced—mate-in-one, forks, pins, and more. Whether you’re new to puzzles or a seasoned player, this is a great way to improve your game in a fun, collaborative setting.

events.title: Crochet Circle BLMI x CYS
events.description:
Join us for our biweekly crochet circle designed for students to unwind and connect. Beyond crafting, we will engage in meaningful contemplation and cultural exploration through the art of fiber. Whether you're a beginner or an expert, come stitch together a stronger community.
Register:
https://cglink.me/2tw/r384123

events.title: Ides of March
events.description: Let’s make the Ides of March a day to remember! Join BC Classical Society March 19th for food and trivia, and a few rounds of pin the dagger on the Julius Caesar!

events.title: Badminton Club Meeting
events.description: Go play

events.title: Junction Social Mixer
events.description: Students are able to mingle with one another and ask senior interns questions about life as an English major or minor at Brooklyn College. It's also a great resource for anyone interested in learning more about the Junction Journal and all the activities hosted by the Riverrun club.

events.title: Curve Stitching
events.description: Academic Event

events.title: Dream Big
events.description: a career development initiative designed to empower students as they prepare for their professional journeys. we are bringing in a network of professional volunteers to provide one-on-one support for students. Students will receive hands-on guidance with resumes, cover letters, and LinkedIn profiles, helping them feel more confident and prepared as they navigate career and internship opportunities.

events.title: Boquet Making
events.description: This is an event where we will be putting together flower bouquets. There will be a kosher meat meal, and we will be having a guest speaker.

events.title: March Motormouths
events.description:
Do you have a topic you love talking about? Do you wish you had an audience that were basically obligated to listen to you nerd-out about a specific interest? Or, would you like to learn something new through listening to a passionate presentation? Then this event is for you!

Join the BC Classical and Historical Societies March 24th for our March Motormouths in the Costas Library (Boylan 2405)! Give a presentation on any topic you desire (under ten minutes). It could even be a presentation for a class that you want some extra practice on! Or, come and listen to a variety of presentations; everyone is welcome!

Food and drinks will be provided!

Please fill out this form if you are interested in presenting at the March Motormouths: https://forms.gle/5md77zySHb2oXa6Y7

Note: You do NOT need to have a presentation to come to the Motormouths event. Feel free to pop in and be an observer! :)

events.title: Open Mic Spring 2026
events.description:
The Riverrun club invites students across the campus community to share original works of poetry, prose, music, or comedy in a supportive environment. The Open Mic is open to all majors. This event is historically hosted every semester by the Riverrun club.

To perform, sign up with the link below. Spots are first come, first serve. Performers must adhere to the guidelines outlined in the sign-up form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeRIaOB5WW-Ot3v2CxBMtaSqgYm_V5iO5JlavoTgxTa6281Mg/viewform?usp=dialog

events.title: TEDxCUNY x USG tabling
events.room: Outside Library Cafe & In Boylan Cafe
events.description: TEDxCUNY is tabling to promote their upcoming Playback Conference in collaboration with CUNY campuses, 'Rewind, Reflect, Reimagine'

events.title: Casual Chess Spring '26
events.description: Looking to relax and play a few games? Join us for our Casual Chess Event! No timers, no pressure - just open boards and friendly matches. Meet fellow players of all levels, share strategies, or simply enjoy a game at your own pace. Bring a friend or challenge someone new!

events.title: Mario Game Fest
events.description: Come celebrate the most iconic gaming legend with games, food, and fun!

events.title: Women’s Karaoke Night
events.description: In honor of woman’s history month we will gather for an evening of celebration and song. We aim to create a safe space for all women to enjoy bracelet making, coloring and of course karaoke.

events.title: Karaoke Knight
events.description: Join us for a Night of Karaoke as we relax after Midterms and enjoy some Wings and Fries while watching Comic nerds get out of their comfort zones and sing the night away

events.title: The Thin Place
events.description:
PERFORMANCE:
Linda and Hilda explore the boundary between life and death, but in order to determine what is real and what isn’t, they must first define reality.
Lucas Hnath’s eerie and thought-provoking drama raises the question: What makes us believe in something we can’t see—and what makes us doubt?

Get Tickets:
https://tickets.brooklyn.edu/eventperformances.asp?evt=496
